vulnerability.title: Disable Kernel Parameter for Accepting Source-Routed Packets on all IPv4 Interfaces
vulnerability.description: To set the runtime status of the net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_source_route kernel parameter, run the following command: $ sudo sysctl -w net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_source_route=0 To make sure that the setting is persistent, add the following line to a file in the directory /etc/sysctl.d: net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_source_route = 0
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then # Comment out any occurrences of net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_source_route from /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf files for f in /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf /run/sysctl.d/*.conf /usr/local/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf; do # skip systemd-sysctl symlink (/etc/sysctl.d/99-sysctl.conf -> /etc/sysctl.conf) if [[ "$(readlink -f "$f")" == "/etc/sysctl.conf" ]]; then continue; fi matching_list=$(grep -P '^(?!#).*[\s]*net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_source_route.*$' $f | uniq ) if ! test -z "$matching_list"; then while IFS= read -r entry; do escaped_entry=$(sed -e 's|/|\\/|g' <<< "$entry") # comment out "net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_source_route" matches to preserve user data sed -i --follow-symlinks "s/^${escaped_entry}$/# &/g" $f done <<< "$matching_l

triage_decision.reason: Disabling source routing is a standard security hardening step that does not affect authentication, authorization, or critical services. It only changes a kernel parameter and is safe to apply.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: networking affected; Potential loss of connectivity for legacy applications that rely on source routing, but this is rare and can be tested before deployment.

reasoning_messages.reasoning:
We need to produce plan then review. According to developer instructions: first we output PLAN: describing the fix (files, values, commands). Then we need to call review_plan. So we first produce a plan description string. Then call review_plan.

Thus proceed step-by-step: output plan description then call review_plan.

Let's propose the plan: modify /etc/sysctl.d/99-disable-source-route.conf or use existing file. Check if file exists. We'll read /etc/sysctl.d/99-sysctl.conf? That file may contain settings. But our simplest: create a new file in /etc/sysctl.d/60-disable-source-route.conf with the line. Ensure no duplicate. Also comment out any existing occurrences in /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf, /run/sysctl.d/*.conf, etc. For that, we can use sed to comment lines starting with the key. But rule: Do not modify sshd_config or firewall. It's safe.

We cannot chain commands. We'll only do one command at a time.

So plan: 1) Read /etc/sysctl.conf and all *.conf in /etc/sysctl.d to check existing value; comment out if present, using sed -i. 2) Write new file /etc/sysctl.d/60-disable-source-route.conf.

Implementation: use read_file to read each file, use sed -i to comment out lines: `sed -i 's/^\s*net\.ipv4\.conf\.all\.accept_source_route[^#]/#net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_source_route/' file`? Actually we want to comment out lines containing key not commented. pattern: `^\s*net\.ipv4\.conf\.all\.accept_source_route\b.*$` and not starting with '#'. Replace with '#net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_source_route\t= 0' or just comment.

Thus we need to call read_file on each file individually? Possibly iterate but each call must be separate. We can't use loops. But we can consider a single command: `sed -i 's/^\s*net\.ipv4\.conf\.all\.accept_source_route\b.*$/#net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_source_route/' /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf` But it's a shell that expands *.conf; that's okay. But we cannot chain multiple sed patterns? Single command is fine. However mixture of files expansions might produce many commands? It's fine.

But environment might not allow globs; but likely yes.

Simpler: use sed -i to comment out in all relevant directories. Use command that runs over files: `sed -i -e 's/^\s*net\.ipv4\.conf\.all\.accept_source_route\b.*$/#net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_source_route/' /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf /run/sysctl.d/*.conf /usr/local/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf 2>/dev/null`.

But practice: some shells may not expand /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf if none. But we can just run sed with path support.

After that, create /etc/sysctl.d/60-disable-source-route.conf with the line.

We need to read file before writing to ensure no duplicate; but we will write new file so check if exists: read_file. If not exist, write; if exists ensure line present.

Thus plan steps:

1) Use read_file to check if `/etc/sysctl.d/60-disable-source-route.conf` exists. If read_file returns error? The tool read_file likely returns content only if exists; maybe empty if not. We may need to attempt read_file and handle if missing. But for plan we just mention writing file with line flush.

2) sed command to comment out existing lines in all .conf.

3) write_file to create /etc/sysctl.d/60-disable-source-route.conf with line.

We also need to ensure runtime - we may run `sysctl -w net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_source_route=0` but that may not be required? In order to make setting persistent, just need config file. But maybe setting change runtime? The finding just requires disabling parameter; but to be safe run sysctl -w. But not necessary? Usually yes.

Thus we could run `sysctl -w net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_source_route=0` after writing config file.

But we must use exactly one command at a time. We can run it as separate command.

Plan summary: comment out any existing lines in sysctl.d directories; create config file; apply runtime; no duplicate lines.

Now produce plan description string.
